summary_en: 7.5M shares (3.14% of S/O) to be acquired for up to ¥30.0B via open-market purchase on TSE, May 18–Oct 29, 2026. Minimum annual dividend floor ¥100/share maintained; 6-year cumulative payout ratio target raised to 50%+ (FY2025–2030).
title_en: Notice on Change to Shareholder Return Policy and Determination of Matters Relating to Acquisition of Treasury Stock
translation_en_brief:
Hankyu Hanshin Holdings' board approved a share buyback programme on May 15, 2026. The company will acquire up to 7.5 million ordinary shares (3.14% of shares outstanding, excluding treasury) for a maximum of ¥30.0 billion via open-market purchase on the Tokyo Stock Exchange between May 18 and October 29, 2026. The acquisition is intended to enhance shareholder returns and improve capital efficiency.

Concurrently, the company revised its shareholder return policy effective from the fiscal year ending March 2026. The new policy maintains a minimum annual dividend of ¥100 per share. The key change is that the total payout ratio target shifts from a flexible annual benchmark of 50% to a cumulative target of 50% or above over the six-year period from fiscal 2025 through fiscal 2030, allowing more tactical deployment of buybacks in response to cash flow conditions and share price movements through the end of fiscal 2030.
